findings.summary: Hades flight controller - 208 components with zero signal detections due to KH-016 legacy wire-to-pin matching failure

correct.description: Component enumeration correct: 208 total with plausible type breakdown (71 caps, 59 resistors, 12 ICs, 20 connectors)
Power rails identified: +3.3V, +3V3, +5V, GND, VCC - reasonable for a flight controller
377 nets and 491 wires parsed from legacy format
Component type classification reasonable: ferrite beads (6), test points (4), jumpers (4) correctly categorized

incorrect.description: Dual naming of 3.3V rail as both +3.3V and +3V3 suggests possible net aliasing issue in the legacy parser

missed.description: Zero signal analysis results - no voltage dividers, RC filters, regulators, crystals, or any other signal patterns detected on a 208-component flight controller board
Flight controller should have: IMU (accelerometer/gyroscope), barometer, voltage regulators, motor driver outputs, RC receiver input - none detected
No design observations generated at all
12 ICs not analyzed for any signal paths - likely includes STM32 MCU, IMU sensors, regulators

suggestions: Fix KH-016 legacy wire-to-pin coordinate matching to enable signal analysis on this board
This board is a good regression test case for KH-016 fixes - expect dozens of signal detections once wiring works

findings.summary: Gerber review: 4-layer flight controller (100x60mm). Drill unit bug + smd_apertures=0

correct.description: All 11 layers present, 4-layer stackup correct from X2. Separate PTH/NPTH drill files correctly identified
382 vias (0.3/0.4mm) correctly classified. 8 NPTH press-fit holes at 0.8mm identified

incorrect.description: Drill extent coordinates not normalized to mm -- values 1000x too large. Affects alignment.layer_extents for drill entries
pad_summary.smd_apertures=0 and smd_ratio=0.0 wrong -- F.Paste has hundreds of flashes
